# Generated Component Anatomy

Annotated breakdown of a liveicons React component generated by `scripts/generate.ts`.
This is what `pnpm generate --icon bell` produces.

```tsx
"use client";
// ↑ Injected by tsup banner (esbuildOptions). Required for RSC compatibility.
//   Do NOT add this manually to animation defs — it's automatic.

// Auto-generated by scripts/generate.ts — source: lucide
// DO NOT edit this file. To modify the animation, edit scripts/animations/bell.ts
// then re-run: pnpm generate --icon bell

import { motion, useAnimation } from "motion/react";
import type { Variants } from "motion/react";
import { forwardRef, useCallback, useEffect, useImperativeHandle, useRef } from "react";
import type { LiveIconProps, LiveIconHandle } from "../types";
import { resolveSpeed } from "@liveicons/core";
// ↑ resolveSpeed maps "slow"|"normal"|"fast"|number → seconds

// Animation variants — defined in scripts/animations/bell.ts
const SVG_VARIANTS: Variants = {
// ↑ Naming convention: {TARGET}_VARIANTS in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
//   "svg" → SVG_VARIANTS
//   "path" → PATH_VARIANTS
//   "group" → GROUP_VARIANTS
  normal: { rotate: 0 },
  animate: { rotate: [0, -12, 12, -8, 8, -4, 0] },
};

const BellIcon = forwardRef<LiveIconHandle, LiveIconProps>(
// ↑ forwardRef enables imperative control via ref.startAnimation() / ref.stopAnimation()
  (
    {
      animate = "on-hover",  // AnimateMode: "on-hover"|"on-click"|"loop"|"once"|false
      speed = "normal",      // AnimateSpeed: "slow"|"normal"|"fast"|number
      size = 24,
      color = "currentColor",
      strokeWidth = 2,
      className,
      onMouseEnter,
      onMouseLeave,
      onClick,
      ...props
    },
    ref
  ) => {
    const controls = useAnimation();
    // ↑ Motion AnimationControls — driven by controls.start("animate") / controls.start("normal")

    const isControlledRef = useRef(false);
    // ↑ Flag: true when parent uses ref.startAnimation() imperatively.
    //   When true, hover/click handlers do NOT auto-trigger.
    //   This prevents conflicts between declarative and imperative control.

    const duration = resolveSpeed(speed);
    // ↑ Computed from speed prop. Passed into transition at render time.
    //   "slow"=1.2s, "normal"=0.6s, "fast"=0.25s

    useEffect(() => {
      if (animate === "loop" || animate === "once") {
        controls.start("animate");
        // ↑ loop/once start immediately on mount.
        // "loop" gets repeat:Infinity injected in the transition below.
        // "once" plays once and stays in animate state.
      }
    }, [animate, controls]);

    useImperativeHandle(ref, () => {
      isControlledRef.current = true;
      // ↑ Setting isControlledRef disables all auto-triggers for this instance.
      return {
        startAnimation: () => controls.start("animate"),
        stopAnimation: () => controls.start("normal"),
      };
    });

    // Hover/click handlers — only fire when not in imperative mode
    const handleMouseEnter = useCallback(
      (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLDivElement>) => {
        if (!isControlledRef.current && animate === "on-hover") {
          controls.start("animate");
        }
        onMouseEnter?.(e);
      },
      [controls, animate, onMouseEnter]
    );

    const handleMouseLeave = useCallback(
      (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLDivElement>) => {
        if (!isControlledRef.current && animate === "on-hover") {
          controls.start("normal");
          // ↑ Returns to normal state on leave. For "on-click" mode this is intentionally skipped.
        }
        onMouseLeave?.(e);
      },
      [controls, animate, onMouseLeave]
    );

    const handleClick = useCallback(
      (e: React.MouseEvent<HTMLDivElement>) => {
        if (!isControlledRef.current && animate === "on-click") {
          controls.start("animate");
        }
        onClick?.(e);
      },
      [controls, animate, onClick]
    );

    return (
      <div
        className={className}
        onMouseEnter={handleMouseEnter}
        onMouseLeave={handleMouseLeave}
        onClick={handleClick}
        {...props}
        // ↑ Spreads all native HTMLDivElement props (style, aria-*, data-*, etc.)
      >
        <motion.svg
          // ↑ SVG target: <motion.svg> wraps everything.
          //   For "path" target, this is plain <svg> with <motion.path> inside.
          animate={controls}
          // ↑ Driven by useAnimation() controls, not the animate prop directly.
          //   This is the Motion way to do imperative + programmatic control.
          xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"
          width={size}
          height={size}
          viewBox="0 0 24 24"
          fill="none"
          stroke={color}
          strokeWidth={strokeWidth}
          strokeLinecap="round"
          strokeLinejoin="round"
          transition={{
            ...{ duration: 0.5, ease: "easeInOut" },  // from animation def
            duration,                                   // overrides with speed prop
            ...(animate === "loop" ? { repeat: Infinity, repeatType: "loop" as const } : {}),
            // ↑ loop:Infinity is injected by the template — never put it in animation defs
          }}
          variants={SVG_VARIANTS}
          // ↑ Motion reads variants.normal and variants.animate by key name
        >
          {/* SVG paths from icons/lucide/bell.svg — extracted and camelCased by generate.ts */}
          <path d="M6 8a6 6 0 0 1 12 0c0 7 3 9 3 9H3s3-2 3-9"/>
          <path d="M10.3 21a1.94 1.94 0 0 0 3.4 0"/>
        </motion.svg>
      </div>
    );
  }
);

BellIcon.displayName = "BellIcon";
// ↑ Required for React DevTools and error messages

export { BellIcon };
export type { LiveIconHandle as BellIconHandle };
// ↑ Re-exported as <ComponentName>Handle for consumers who need the imperative ref type
```

---

## Path target variant (check icon)

When `target: "path"`, the generated structure differs:

```tsx
// PATH_VARIANTS — animated on specific <motion.path> elements
const PATH_VARIANTS: Variants = {
  normal: { opacity: 1, pathLength: 1, scale: 1 },
  animate: { opacity: [0, 1], pathLength: [0, 1], scale: [0.6, 1] },
};

// In the JSX:
<svg xmlns="..." width={size} height={size} viewBox="0 0 24 24" ...>
  <motion.path
    d="M20 6 9 17l-5-5"
    animate={controls}
    initial="normal"          // ← added when target="path"
    variants={PATH_VARIANTS}
    transition={{ ...{ duration: 0.4 }, duration }}
  />
  {/* pathLength requires the path to have pathLength="1" set */}
  {/* OR use SVG's native path length — Motion normalizes automatically */}
</svg>
```

**Key differences from SVG target:**
1. `initial="normal"` is set on the `<motion.path>` (not needed on `<motion.svg>`)
2. `repeat:Infinity` is NOT added to path target transitions even in loop mode — path animations typically don't loop
3. `pathLength` values animate 0→1 for draw-on effect; Motion handles path length normalization

---

## Advanced: staggered multi-path draw

When an icon has multiple paths that should draw in sequentially (not currently in the generator, but possible to hand-write), use the `custom` prop with a variant function:

```tsx
const pathVariants = {
  normal: { pathLength: 0, opacity: 0 },
  animate: (i: number) => ({
    pathLength: 1,
    opacity: 1,
    transition: {
      pathLength: { duration: 0.4, delay: i * 0.15 },
      opacity:    { duration: 0.1, delay: i * 0.15 },
    },
  }),
};

// In JSX — each path gets its stagger index via `custom`:
<motion.path custom={0} variants={pathVariants} animate={controls} initial="normal" d="..." />
<motion.path custom={1} variants={pathVariants} animate={controls} initial="normal" d="..." />
```

---

## Performance notes

- Only `transform` and `opacity` are GPU-composited. `stroke-dashoffset` (which Motion uses internally for `pathLength`) triggers **repaint** each frame — acceptable for 24×24 icons but keep animated path complexity under ~200 points.
- `will-change` is handled dynamically by the hook — never set it globally in CSS on all SVG elements.
- Motion sets `transform-box: fill-box` automatically on all `motion.*` SVG elements, so `rotate` is always element-relative, not viewBox-origin-relative.
